characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The main character of the series and the quintessential50severyman. Richie is the second-eldest Cunningham child and son of Marion and Howard, and older brother of Joanie. His relationship with Lori Beth is a recurring series element beginning with Season 5. He wasPut on a Busduring Season 8, but came back in the final season. Allegedly Dateless: Despite his occasional self-proclaimed "dry spells" with women, he is often shown to be successful for the most part in the dating game. Aloof Older Brother: To Joanie. Despite beingprotective of her when she's in fate's hands, Richie hasn't much closeness to Joanie and they're rarely shown bonding together over shared interest. This is usually because Richie would be at Arnold's with the guys or out with Lori Beth, or experienced personal matters with his friends or parents which reduced the time he has to hang with Joanie. In any other case, it'd have been due to Joanie makingsassy remarksdirected at him. Back for the Finale: Reappeared at the very end of the final episode (along with Lori Beth) to attend Joanie and Chachi's wedding. Beware the Nice Ones: If provoked, he would become angry or annoyed.As Fonzie puts it, "If he's calling you 'bucko'...you're inbigtrouble.

characters.name: Richie Cunningham
characters.description: The main character of the series and the quintessential50severyman. Richie is the second-eldest Cunningham child and son of Marion and Howard, and older brother of Joanie. His relationship with Lori Beth is a recurring series element beginning with Season 5. He wasPut on a Busduring Season 8, but came back in the final season. Allegedly Dateless: Despite his occasional self-proclaimed "dry spells" with women, he is often shown to be successful for the most part in the dating game. Aloof Older Brother: To Joanie. Despite beingprotective of her when she's in fate's hands, Richie hasn't much closeness to Joanie and they're rarely shown bonding together over shared interest. This is usually because Richie would be at Arnold's with the guys or out with Lori Beth, or experienced personal matters with his friends or parents which reduced the time he has to hang with Joanie. In any other case, it'd have been due to Joanie makingsassy remarksdirected at him. Back for the Finale: Reappeared at the very end of the final episode (along with Lori Beth) to attend Joanie and Chachi's wedding. Beware the Nice Ones: If provoked, he would become angry or annoyed.As Fonzie puts it, "If he's calling you 'bucko'...you're inbigtrouble.

characters.name: Marion Cunningham
characters.description: The loving mother of Richie, Joanie, and Chuck, and wife of Howard. Marion is ahousewifewho does most of the cooking and cleaning in addition to nurturing the kids. She doesn't have a regular job, though she has worked at Arnold's as a waitress on at least one occasion. All-Loving Hero: Probably the nicest character on the show. She is very nice towards all of Richie's friends and shows concern even for people who don't deserve it. Berserk Button: She was the only character ever to tell Fonzie to "sit on it." Beware the Nice Ones: She rarely gets mad, but when she does even the Fonz is put on his heels. First-Name Basis: The only one who calls Fonzie by his first name. She's also the only one who calls Potsie by his first name Warren.

characters.name: Howard Cunningham
characters.description: The father of Richie, Joanie, and Chuck and husband of Marion. He's known for being overprotective towards his daughter and getting into disputes with his wife,but he's still a good person deep down. And Starring: "...Tom Bosley as Howard Cunningham." Big Eater: Zigzagged. People say he eats a lot, but he's not nearly as big of an eater as Chuck or Potsie. Birthday Episode: "Howard's 45th Fiasco" focused on him having a midlife crisis on his birthday. Bumbling Dad: At times, although he's smarter than most examples, but sometimes his old-fashioned views and being overprotective towards Joanie give his irrational moments. And as seen below, he also has his moments ofParental Hypocrisywhich are noticed by the rest of his family.

characters.name: Joanie Cunningham
characters.description: Richie's little sister and the only daughter of Marion and Howard Cunningham. Joanie is atypicalteenagegirlwhose defining trait is beingtreated like a childnot just by her family, but by everyone else. This is a great annoyance of Joanie and often leads her to rebel to show that she's more mature than given credit for. Despite hersnarky attitude, she's still aNice Girl. Her relationship with Chachi becomes a major plot theme once they start dating near the end of Season 7,and they end up getting married in the series finale. Aesop Amnesia: All too often Joanie would return to her rebellious ways after her parents had shown her sheCan't Get Away with Nuthin'. The same girl who quit smoking and realized she didn't need to gain popularity among a clique she has nothing in common with later tries to buy a car behind her parents' backs. Affectionate Nickname:"Shortcake" from Fonzie and Arnold.Jenny Piccalo would call her "Joanes".Chachi would call her "Blue Eyes". "Shortcake" from Fonzie and Arnold. Jenny Piccalo would call her "Joanes".

characters.name: Chuck Cunningham
characters.description: Richie and Joanie's older brother who was characterized as aDumb Jockbasketball player leaving for college with what little screentime he had. Eventually vanished from the series and was never acknowledged again, with no in-series explanation provided for his disappearance, thereby making him theTrope NamerofChuck Cunningham Syndrome. Alliterative Name:ChuckCunningham. Aloof Older Brother: Due to being rarely seen, he and Richie are not particularly close. He also doesn't seem to be too close to Joanie, as Joanie makes condescending remarks about hisBook Dumbintelligence at the dinner table. Big Eater: According to his parents, "nobody has the appetite that Chuck has". Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: TheTrope Namer, unfortunately. He ceased making appearances on the series after the second season and was never mentioned again, to the point that his existence isn't even acknowledged by his father at the end of the series.

characters.name: Potsie Weber
characters.description: One of Richie's best friends. His defining characteristic is hisstupidity, though he makes up for it inunexpected musical talent. Advertised Extra: After Richie and Ralph left, Potsie remains on the show but his screen time is greatly reduced because the writers struggled to find things for him to do. He rarely does anything relevant in an episode (he often appears in just one scene) and is entirely absent from several. Despite this, he's still in the opening credits, even in the last season where he only appears in 6 of the 22 episodes, not including the series finale. Alliterative Name:WarrenWeber. The Artifact: After Richie and Ralph left the show. Lampshaded in the episode where Arnold returns to the show.Potsie:I'm still here!Arnold:Why? Butt-Monkey: Often on the receiving end of sarcastic wisecracks.

characters.name: The Fonz
characters.description: The initialTrope NamerforBreakout Character, Fonzie is the famouscasanovaand motorcyclist who's admired for his badassery, sometimes amounting to superhuman powers. He sometimesacts self-obsessed,but his heart is in the right place. His parents ran out on him, forcing him to live with his grandmother until moving into the spare room above the Cunninghams'. Accidental Marriage: With Jenny Piccalo in "Bride and Gloom." Later, it turns out the marriage was not really valid. The Ace: Turns into this around Season 5, with the uncanny ability to come out on top at just aboutanythingfrom water-skiing to dueling. Quite an accomplishment for a high school dropout mechanic. Although, he was always an Ace at repair work. Badass Biker: A family-friendly version; as his role was increased, his badassitude morphed from traditional "tough guy" to near-super-powered nice guy. Badass Boast: Prone to making these, with one such example being "Hey, it's not the uniform, it's the maninthe uniform and remember, the man in this uniformcan break your face!"

characters.name: Ralph Malph
characters.description: One of Richie's friends who's defining trait is being theFun Personifiedjokester. The Bus Came Back: In one episode of the last season, he returned after being in the military. Casanova Wannabe: In earlier seasons, his girl-chasing was the primary focus of his character. He would occasionally get dates but generally he was not very successful at it. Catchphrase: "I still got it!" Characterization Marches On: Early on, Ralph was considerably "cooler" than Richie and Potsie. Around the time Fonzie's popular really started to take off, Ralph was demoted to being as nerdy as Richie and Potsie.

characters.name: Jenny Piccalo
characters.description: Joanie's best friendwhom she has known ever since the age of six. Was originally anunseen characteronly mentioned whenever Joanie was going over to her place or calling her over the phone, before finallymaking her first on-screen appearance in Season 8, where she's portrayed now as a boy-crazy ditz rather than the cool girl she was made out to be to Joanie in her past mentions. All Women Are Lustful: She is lustful and expects females in general to be. She's also the one who taught Joanie that divorced ladies were "hot to trot". Attention Whore: Seen in many episodes, like when she desperately tries to impress aGirl Posse. Back for the Finale: She was cut from the cast in the final season, but returns as a guest star in the series finale. Characterization Marches On: When she was justThe Ghost, Jenny was painted as Joanie's cool and "experienced" friend. However, after she actually appears on the show and emerges as a major character, they made her a delusional and ditzySmall Name, Big Egowho makes Joanie look better.

characters.name: "Bag" Zambroni
characters.description: A member of the local Demons gang. We're first introduced to him when Richie and Potsie try to join the Demons; though they ultimately strike out at membership, he ends up becoming their friend (and the only member of the Demons to make recurring appearances). Also the original drummer for Richie, Potsie, and Ralph's band. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Disappeared between seasons two and three. Gentle Giant: In spite of his size and being a member of the Devils, he's fairly nice to Richie and friends. Only Known by Their Nickname: We're never told what his real first name is. Vitriolic Best Buds: With Richie, Ralph, and Potsie.

characters.name: "Sticks" Downey
characters.description: Long-Lost Uncle Aesop: In "Fonzie's New Friend", he taught people not to be racist, but never appeared afterwards. Shoo Out the New Guy: He only made one appearance. Token Black Friend: He is black and is Fonzie's pal, but only appeared to teach an anti-prejudiceAesop. You Know I'm Black, Right?: An entire episode revolves around how the entire town is racist towards him, much to the chagrin of Fonzie.

characters.name: Eugene Belvin
characters.description: A student of Fonzie's after the latter becomes a teacher at Jefferson High. Along with his brother Melvin, they're the first real stereotypical "nerds" on the show — complete with glasses, pocket protectors, an interest in science, andabsolutely no respect from their classmates (or teachers). Abhorrent Admirer: To Jenny. Butt-Monkey: He's picked on by all his classmates.

characters.name: Arnold Takahashi
characters.description: The original owner of Arnold's (though his name isn'tactuallyArnold). Gets married to a Japanese woman named "Momo" in the season three finale, and sells Arnold's to Al Delvecchio not long after. The Bus Came Back: He went off to live with his new bride at the end of season three, then came back for guest spots in seasons five and seven before returning and reassuming his position as owner of Arnold's in season 10. But Not Too Foreign: He has adapted to many of America's cultural attributes, but still embraces his native Japan. Catchphrase: Not really a catchphrase, per se, but he did have a habit of making a "frrmp" sound when exasperated. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: After an extended absence, he came back and reassumed his position as owner of Arnold's in season 10. Then, after the first episode of season 11, he vanished again, this time with no explanation.

characters.name: Al Delvecchio
characters.description: The second owner of Arnold's, immediately following Arnold himself. In season 10 he falls in love with and marries Chachi's mother Louisa Arcola, and leavesHappy Daysfor the spinoffJoanie Loves Chachi. Back for the Finale: He comes back in three episodes of the last season, including the finale. Catchphrase: "Yeeppp, yep, yep, yep, yep, yep..." whenever he was disappointed or things did not go his way. Extreme Doormat: He can rarely ever say "no" to anything. He finally stands up for himself in the first episode ofJoanie Loves Chachi. Iconic Sequel Character: Due to his longevity and popularity, he ultimately becomes just as iconic, if not more so, than the character he replaced (Arnold)

characters.name: Marsha Simms
characters.description: A waitress at Arnold's. Butt-Monkey: Often gets pranked by Ralph. Hair of Gold, Heart of Gold: Pretty reasonable and blonde.

characters.name: Roger Phillips
characters.description: Richie's cousin, who arrives in season eight and starts out as the coach of Jefferson High, then becomes principal of the troubled Patton High. Like Richie, he's a nice, intelligent guy who believes in doing things by the book;unlike Richie, he's stiff as a board and prone to moments of arrogance. Cool Teacher: The coach of Jefferson High and later, the principal of Patton. Flat Character: Unlike Richie, who was anEndearingly Dorkyand realistically flawed everyman, Roger is just a generic nephew who just happens to be a teacher, without any significant personality traits. Foolish Sibling, Responsible Sibling: The Responsible to Flip's Foolish. Promotion to Opening Titles: In Season 10.

characters.name: Flip Phillips
characters.description: Roger's younger brother. Annoying Younger Sibling: To Roger. Arson, Murder, and Jaywalking: In "I Drink, Therefore I Am" when he accidentally injures Heather while driving intoxicated. Foolish Sibling, Responsible Sibling: The Foolish to Roger's Responsible. Suspiciously Similar Substitute: For Chachi.

characters.name: K.C. Cunningham
characters.description: Another Cunningham cousin, this time the daughter of Howard's brother. An upbeat, if simple, young girl with a Southern drawl whom Jenny Piccalo often takes under her wing in the dating scene. Bad Date: With Melvin Belvin. Beware the Nice Ones: She would get mad when provoked. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: She disappeared without explanation after Season 10. Nice Girl: A very friendly girl.

characters.name: Chachi Arcola
characters.description: Fonzie's cousin who starts out as a young, aspiring salesman wannabe. Infatuated with Joanie from the start, he finally successfully wins her over as he matures into a young adult, eventually moving to Chicago with her in the spinoffJoanie Loves Chachi, before the two of them end up back in Milwaukee.He marries her in the series finale. Abhorrent Admirer: Until the end of Season 7, Joanie would be repulsed by Chachi's attempts to impress her and disgusted by him hitting on her, responding to his attempts witha snarky rejection. Ascended Extra: Starts out as a recurring character in season 5, becomes a regular in season 6, and by the time Richie isPut on a Bus(season 8), he's pretty much theDeuteragonist. The Bus Came Back: Made sporadic guest appearances onHappy Daysafter departing forJoanie Loves Chachi, then came back full time for the final season after the latter series was cancelled. Catchphrase: "Wa, wa, wa." Dropped as he grew up.

characters.name: Spike Fonzarelli
characters.description: Fonzie's 14-year-old cousin, but for some reason he wants to be called a nephew. Can best be described as a clone of Fonzie, right down to the leather jacket, greased-back hair, and thumbs-up attitude... but unlike Fonzie, he has an interest in botany. Berserk Button: Donottouch the hair. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Disappears after season four with no explanation, and is by and large replaced by Chachi. Embarrassing First Name: His real first name is Raymond, which Fonzie says he's "trying to live down". Hates Being Touched: Doesn't like when people touch either him or his hair.

characters.name: Grandma Nussbaum
characters.description: Fonzie's grandmother.

characters.name: Lori Beth Allen
characters.description: Richie's girlfriend introduced in Season 5. Eventually becomes married to him. Babies Make Everything Better: She gives birth to Richie Jr. in "Little Baby Cunningham", although the baby isn't given much of an emphasis on episodes, disappearing after Lynda Goodfriend left the cast in Season 10, and returning as a fully-grown toddler named Richie, Jr. Back for the Finale: Lori Beth and Richie returned in the series finale "Passages", where they attended Joanie and Chachi's wedding. The Bus Came Back: After beingPut on a Busfor season 10, she came back with Richie for the final season episodes "Welcome Home (Parts 1 & 2)" and "Passages". Demoted to Extra: When Richie left to join the Army in Greenland, she stuck around, getting married to him over the phone, and giving birth to Richie's son when he was away. Eventually, she pretty much just stopped appearing entirely unless it was a special occasion, having little way to play off the others.

characters.name: Pinky Tuscadero
characters.description: A biker who almost married Fonzie and has two assistants named the Pinkettes. Has a motif around the colour pink and is quite fiery. Catchphrase: "Think pink!", although it was more said by her assistants. Color-Coded Characters: Has a pink theme. Love Interest: There's even a whole episode called "Fonzie Loves Pinky". The Maiden Name Debate: Inverted when she wanted Fonzie to changehisname, so he called off the wedding.

characters.name: Ashley Pfister
characters.description: Fonzie's other most significant and longest-lasting girlfriend, introduced in Season 10. Glamorous Single Mother: Though she occasionally shows signs of stress and fatigue, she manages to juggle a relationship with Fonzie, a full-time job, and a just-as-full-time parenting responsibility with her daughter Heather — all with never a hair out of place. Love Interest: For Fonzie. Put on a Bus: After Season 10, Purl left the show becauseHappy Dayshad to cut back on its number of cast members. Ashley's sudden disappearance is explained by Fonzie that she had departed with her daughter to get back together with her husband.

characters.name: The Aloha Pussycats
characters.description: A Hawaiian dance troupe. Head-Turning Beauty: Fonzie dates them, and Richie, Potsie, Ralph, and evenAl, have crushes on them. No Name Given: We don't find out their individual names.

characters.name: The Hooper Triplets
characters.description: Three of Fonzie's love interests. The Ghost: They're mentioned a lot, but never seen. Love Interest: Have dated Fonzie.

characters.name: Officer Kirk
characters.description: A police officer who has it out for Fonzie. Jerkass: He's arrogant, boisterous and controlling. Needless to say, no one in town likes him, especially Fonzie. The Neidermeyer: A non-military example, though he does become an Army Reserve Major in later seasons. Kirk acts more like a overzealous Army Major than a small-town police officer, not to mention he is borderline fascistic and overall an unpleasant person. Tyrant Takes the Helm: He briefly becomes acting sherriff after the untimely passing of the previous sherriff in "A.K.A. The Fonz."

characters.name: Heather Pfister
characters.description: Ashley Pfister's daughter. Put on a Bus: After Season 10, O'Rourke's character was written out because the show had to cut back on its number of cast members. Her disappearance was explained that she and her mother had gone back in search of her father.

characters.name: Spunky
characters.description: Dogs Love Fire Hydrants: In "Spunky Come Home", Potsie wonders if she's sniffing fire hydrants. Tropey, Come Home: She went missing in "Spunky Come Home".
